task_completion.description: Verify the location is set to Phoenix, AZ and the PV system size is configured to 5.0 kW.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the SAM interface showing 'Phoenix' in the Location and Resource page, and '5.0' (or very close to it) for the System Nameplate Capacity on the System Design page.

task_completion.description: Verify the battery capacity is set to 10.0 kWh and the power rating is 5.0 kW.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the SAM Battery Storage page showing a total battery capacity of approximately 10.0 kWh and a maximum power/inverter rating of 5.0 kW.

task_completion.description: Verify the battery replacement is configured at an 80% capacity threshold with a cost of $300 per kWh.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the SAM Battery Life or System Costs pages showing 'Replace at capacity threshold' set to 80%, and battery replacement costs set to 300 $/kWh.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of two distinct scenarios or cases for Lead-Acid (AGM) and Lithium-Ion (LFP).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for multiple case tabs in the SAM interface (e.g., 'Lead-Acid' and 'Lithium-Ion') or a PySAM script defining two distinct battery chemistry models.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation and content of the required JSON file containing the NPV and replacement costs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing 'battery_chemistry_comparison.json' with the required structure: 'system_size_kw', 'battery_capacity_kwh', and a 'scenarios' object containing 'lead_acid' and 'lithium_ion_lfp' with their respective 'npv_dollars' and 'pv_battery_replacement_cost_dollars'.

task_completion.description: Verify the SAM project file or PySAM script is saved to the correct directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager, terminal, or save dialog showing 'battery_comparison.sam' or 'compare_batteries.py' located in the '/home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/' directory.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the NREL System Advisor Model (SAM) GUI or wrote a functional PySAM script to generate the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the SAM application open with the relevant parameters entered, or a Python script importing and using PySAM modules, rather than just a text editor mocking the final JSON.

integrity.description: Verify the NPV and battery replacement costs in the JSON file appear to be genuine simulation results.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at the JSON file contents; the 'npv_dollars' and 'pv_battery_replacement_cost_dollars' should be specific, non-zero numerical values (likely with decimals) rather than placeholder text like '<value>' or generic round numbers.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not write a script that simply prints hardcoded JSON without running a simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: If a Python script is used, ensure it contains simulation logic (e.g., executing a model, reading outputs) and not just a 'json.dump()' of manually typed values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected JSON filename: battery_chemistry_comparison.json. Expected SAM project filename: battery_comparison.sam OR PySAM script: compare_batteries.py. Expected PV system size: 5.0 kW (acceptable range 4.9-5.1). Expected battery capacity: 10.0 kWh (acceptable range 9.5-10.5).
